description: You are an oral and maxillofacial surgeon preparing a comprehensive pre-operative bilateral symmetry workup for a patient presenting with suspected facial asymmetry who is being evaluated for orthognathic surgery. Using the CT imaging series loaded in InVesalius 3, complete a full bilateral craniofacial asymmetry analysis. Create a bone segmentation mask using appropriate Hounsfield thresholds (typically above 226 HU) and generate a 3D bone surface mesh. You must place at least 12 linear measurements following a bilateral protocol, including at least 4 bilateral pairs at symmetric anatomical locations (such as temporal width, orbital width, mastoid process height, or parietal width) and 4 midline/overall cranial measurements (such as maximum cranial length, breadth, height, or minimum frontal breadth). Export the 3D bone surface as a binary STL file to /home/ga/Documents/asymmetry_study/skull_model.stl. Capture and save screenshots of the 3D surface from five standard anatomical orientations: anterior (/home/ga/Documents/asymmetry_study/anterior_view.png), left lateral (/home/ga/Documents/asymmetry_study/left_lateral.png), right lateral (/home/ga/Documents/asymmetry_study/right_lateral.png), superior (/home/ga/Documents/asymmetry_study/superior_view.png), and posterior (/home/ga/Documents/asymmetry_study/posterior_view.png). Save the complete InVesalius project, including the bone mask, 3D surface, and all measurements, to /home/ga/Documents/asymmetry_study/asymmetry_analysis.inv3. The surgical assessment form is located at /home/ga/Documents/asymmetry_study/assessment_form.txt. Login credentials: username=ga, password=password123
